cmd:
set -e
BIN=/var/lib/easyenclave/bin
SRC=$BIN/podman-linux-amd64
DATA_DIR=/var/lib/easyenclave/data
until [ -x $SRC/usr/local/bin/podman ]; do sleep 1; done
# Wait for mount-data to mount /dev/vdc before writing persistent
# container state. The root filesystem is read-only, so /data may not
# exist; use the writable easyenclave state directory as the mountpoint.
# On VMs without vdc (e.g. CP previews with no workload disk) this
# check short-circuits and podman falls back to vfs below.
if [ -b /dev/vdc ]; then
  until mountpoint -q "$DATA_DIR" 2>/dev/null; do sleep 1; done
fi
mkdir -p "$DATA_DIR"
# Stage helpers first (conmon, netavark, crun, etc.).
for f in $SRC/usr/local/bin/*; do
  name=$(basename $f)
  case $name in
    podman) cp -f $f $BIN/.podman-raw ;;
    *)      cp -f $f $BIN/ ;;
  esac
done
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon $BIN/
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/netavark $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/aardvark-dns $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/rootlessport $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
# Some EE filesystems/assets do not preserve executable bits reliably across
# release-asset unpack + staging. Make the delegated binary and helpers
# executable explicitly, then fail bootstrap if the raw binary cannot exec.
chmod +x $BIN/.podman-raw $BIN/conmon $BIN/crun 2>/dev/null || true
for helper in netavark aardvark-dns rootlessport slirp4netns pasta; do
  [ -e "$BIN/$helper" ] && chmod +x "$BIN/$helper" || true
done
mkdir -p "$DATA_DIR/.podman"
# /dev/shm holds podman's per-container POSIX shm lock file
# (libpod_lock). EE may not mount tmpfs there; without it, podman
# fails `failed to create 2048 locks in /libpod_lock`. Idempotent.
if ! mountpoint -q /dev/shm 2>/dev/null; then
  mkdir -p /dev/shm
  mount -t tmpfs -o size=64M tmpfs /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true
fi
# Pick storage driver by probing kernel overlayfs on the actual graphroot
# backing filesystem. Some extfs-looking EE mounts still reject overlay,
# so mountpoint alone is not enough.
DRIVER=vfs
if mountpoint -q "$DATA_DIR" 2>/dev/null && grep -qw overlay /proc/filesystems 2>/dev/null; then
  PROBE=$DATA_DIR/.podman/overlay-probe
  mkdir -p "$PROBE/lower" "$PROBE/upper" "$PROBE/work" "$PROBE/merged"
  : > "$PROBE/lower/.probe"
  if mount -t overlay overlay -o lowerdir="$PROBE/lower",upperdir="$PROBE/upper",workdir="$PROBE/work" "$PROBE/merged" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    umount "$PROBE/merged" 2>/dev/null || true
    DRIVER=overlay
  fi
  rm -rf "$PROBE"
fi
STORAGE_ROOT=$DATA_DIR/.podman/storage-$DRIVER
RUNROOT=$DATA_DIR/.podman/runroot-$DRIVER
mkdir -p "$STORAGE_ROOT" "$RUNROOT"
POL=$DATA_DIR/.podman/policy.json
printf '%s' '{"default":[{"type":"insecureAcceptAnything"}]}' > $POL
# /etc and /root are RO on EE. Build a writable fake HOME for
# policy.json + podman's default lookups.
HOME_DIR=/var/lib/easyenclave/.home
mkdir -p $HOME_DIR/.config/containers $HOME_DIR/tmp
cp -f $POL $HOME_DIR/.config/containers/policy.json
CONF=$DATA_DIR/.podman/containers.conf
printf '%s\n' '[engine]' 'helper_binaries_dir = ["/var/lib/easyenclave/bin"]' > $CONF
# Wrapper installed as $BIN/podman so bare `podman ps` (from PATH)
# reaches the right storage root + driver. Raw binary lives at
# $BIN/.podman-raw. $BIN/dd-podman stays as a back-compat symlink
# since openclaw's workload calls dd-podman by name.
printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' "export HOME=$HOME_DIR" "export TMPDIR=$HOME_DIR/tmp" "export CONTAINERS_CONF=$CONF" 'export PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING=1' "exec $BIN/.podman-raw --conmon=$BIN/conmon --runtime=$BIN/crun --storage-driver=$DRIVER --root=$STORAGE_ROOT --runroot=$RUNROOT --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs \"\$@\"" > $BIN/podman
chmod +x $BIN/podman
ln -sf podman $BIN/dd-podman
ls -la $CONF $POL $BIN/podman $BIN/dd-podman $BIN/.podman-raw 2>&1 || true
$BIN/.podman-raw --version >/dev/null
echo podman-bootstrap: ok driver=$DRIVER data=$DATA_DIR root=$STORAGE_ROOT runroot=$RUNROOT conf=$CONF policy=$POL
